rulings.Case Title: CAROLE WALSH et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant 3M Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE EGGLETON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Eggleton was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Order Granting Leave To File 2nd Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK LEADERICH et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that: 1) John Leaderich was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant; 2) defendant manufactured, sold or supplied a defective product that Mr. Leaderich was exposed to; 3) defendant made an actionable false representation; and/or 4) defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Defendant¿s faillure to demonstrate that plaintiffs responded to written contention discovery precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)
